docs.title: orbit4ml
docs.text:
orbit4ml is an open ecosystem for machine learning in space.

Space is the next frontier for intelligent systems — but running ML in orbit, on the lunar surface, or deep in the solar system is fundamentally different from running it in a terrestrial data center. orbit4ml brings together the libraries, tools, and frameworks needed to navigate that gap.

docs.title: Why orbit4ml?
docs.text:
Deploying ML in space introduces a unique set of constraints that terrestrial frameworks are not designed for:

  • Compute — radiation-hardened processors run at a fraction of the speed and capability of modern GPUs. FPGAs and ASICs dominate.
  • Bandwidth — downlink windows are short and expensive. Models must process data on-board and transmit only what matters.
  • Latency — the speed of light makes real-time ground control loops impossible beyond LEO. Autonomy is not optional.
  • Data scarcity — labeled datasets from space missions are rare. Few-shot and meta-learning approaches are essential.
  • Reliability — cosmic ray bit flips, thermal cycling, and single-event upsets demand fault-tolerant training and inference.

orbit4ml is built to address these constraints directly, providing implementations and abstractions that space ML practitioners can use out of the box.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
orbit4ml is organized into focused submodules:

  • orbit4ml.data — Datasets, transforms, and task samplers for space imagery, telemetry, and scientific observations.
  • orbit4ml.compress — Model compression, quantization, and pruning pipelines optimized for low-bandwidth uplink/downlink constraints.
  • orbit4ml.edge — Inference engines and model exporters targeting space-grade hardware: FPGAs, radiation-tolerant SoCs, and embedded processors.
  • orbit4ml.adapt — Meta-learning and continual learning algorithms for in-orbit adaptation with minimal data and no ground-in-the-loop.
  • orbit4ml.comm — Utilities for delay-tolerant networking (DTN), intermittent connectivity, and federated learning across satellite constellations.
  • orbit4ml.bench — Standardized benchmarks and evaluation protocols for space ML tasks, enabling fair comparison across methods.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Coming soon

orbit4ml is under active development. The first release will be announced on GitHub.

pip install orbit4ml

docs.title: High-level Wrappers
docs.text:
import orbit4ml

# Coming soon

docs.title: Learning Domains
docs.text:
orbit4ml will ship with ready-to-use learning domains covering:

  • Earth observation (multispectral, SAR, hyperspectral)
  • Spacecraft telemetry anomaly detection
  • Space situational awareness
  • On-board science autonomy

docs.title: Low-Level Utilities
docs.text: # Coming soon

docs.title: Citation
docs.text:
If you use orbit4ml in your research, please cite:

@software{orbit4ml2026,
  author  = {Mainak Mallick},
  title   = {orbit4ml: An Open Ecosystem for Machine Learning in Space},
  year    = {2026},
  url     = {https://github.com/orbit-ml/orbit4ml}
}

docs.title: Acknowledgements & Friends
docs.text: orbit4ml draws inspiration from learn2learn, PyTorch, and the broader open-source ML community. We also draw on the excellent work coming out of space agencies, satellite operators, and the NewSpace community pushing the boundaries of on-board intelligence.

docs.title: Unreleased
docs.text:
• Initial project scaffold and documentation site.
  • Defined submodule architecture: data, compress, edge, adapt, comm, bench.

docs.title: Community
docs.text: orbit4ml is an open project. Contributions, feedback, and discussion are welcome.

docs.title: Get Involved
docs.text:
• GitHub — github.com/orbitml/orbit4ml — open issues, submit PRs, or start a discussion.
  • Discussions — Use GitHub Discussions for questions, ideas, and showcasing your work.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Contribution guidelines will be published with the first release. In the meantime, feel free to open an issue or discussion to share ideas or flag gaps in the ecosystem.

docs.title: Code of Conduct
docs.text: This project follows the Contributor Covenant code of conduct. Be kind, be constructive.

docs.text:
API reference for all orbit4ml submodules.

Coming soon

API documentation will be auto-generated from source docstrings once the first module is released.

docs.title: Submodules
docs.text: Module Description orbit4ml.data Datasets, transforms, and task samplers orbit4ml.compress Model compression for low-bandwidth constraints orbit4ml.edge Inference for space-grade hardware orbit4ml.adapt Meta-learning and continual learning orbit4ml.comm Delay-tolerant and federated learning utilities orbit4ml.bench Benchmarks and evaluation protocols

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
Runnable notebooks and scripts demonstrating orbit4ml in action.

Coming soon

Examples will be added alongside each module release.

docs.title: Planned Examples
docs.text:
• Few-shot classification on satellite imagery
  • Telemetry anomaly detection with limited labels
  • Quantizing a model to run on a radiation-tolerant FPGA
  • Federated learning across a satellite constellation

docs.title: Tutorials
docs.text:
Welcome to the orbit4ml tutorials. These guides walk you through the core concepts and workflows step by step.

Coming soon

Tutorials are being written alongside the first library release. Check back soon or watch the GitHub repo for updates.

docs.title: Planned Tutorials
docs.text:
• Getting Started — Install orbit4ml and run your first experiment.
  • Working with Space Datasets — Load and transform Earth observation and telemetry data.
  • Compressing Models for Downlink — Reduce model size for bandwidth-constrained missions.
  • In-Orbit Adaptation with Meta-Learning — Adapt pre-trained models on-board with minimal data.
  • Benchmarking Your Method — Use orbit4ml.bench to evaluate and compare approaches fairly.
